description.value:
This technique can only be learned if you selected it when you took the Mangekyou Sharingan feat.

You combine powerful illusory powers with the special spacetime distortion capabilities of the Mangekyou Sharingan, which must be active when using this technique. You empower your eyes with Tsukuyomi until you target a creature with it through a gaze attack. The subject will then be taken to an illusory realm within its own mind, where time operates differently. No matter the usage, the subject will be @Condition[stunned;duration=1]{stunned} for 1 round after the illusion ends (Will negates).

A creature can only be subjected to Tsukuyomi once per 24 hour period (in reality), and any ability damage inflicted (as below) takes twice as long to recover. You also gain a +4 bonus to checks to overcome Chakra Resistance with this technique. A creature with the Sharingan ability gains a bonus to its saves to resist this technique equal to its Sharingan ability bonus.

The technique has one of two effects, either to have you relay a message or to have you torture the target.

Either way, the effects lasts only a few seconds in reality, but lasts for up to 1 day in the perception of both yourself and the subject.

Message - You can converse with the subject normally, in an illusory environment.

Torture - You have the subject experience extreme torture, at your own hands or at those of an illusion. The torture can include any kind of mental and/or physical torture, and due to the mental stress it causes the subject may feel like the torture lasts much longer than it actually does. Each 'day' of torture inflicts 1d4+2 permanent Wisdom damage (Will negates), as well as 4d6 damage. The Wisdom damage can only be healed by a character of level 16 or higher, or by a character with at least 8 levels of Medical Specialist. A character who falls to 0 Wisdom or below will slip into a coma, and remains so until their Wisdom is restored to 1 or higher. On the subject's next turn, they take 1d6 damage per 'day' they were tortured.

If the subject survives the torture and did not reach 0 Wisdom, any Wisdom damage inflicted is removed after 24 hours, but the subject will take 1d4 temporary Intelligence and Charisma damage. As a downside, you suffer 1 mangekyou sharingan blindness if you torture the subject for more than a day, and an additional 6 mangekyou sharingan blind-ness if you torture the subject for more than three days.

Empower: Spend 6 Chakra to increase the duration of the technique by 1 day (inside the illusion, realworld duration unchanged), up to a maximum of 4 days total.

Mastery: The fifth step increases the maximum number of days to 5. If you choose to torture a creature for five days, you suffer an additional 6 points of mange-kyou sharingan blindness.
